summary: Closed-unmerged lbmemory matching PR that introduced typed allocator/layout fields, explicit data and sdata string symbols, and decompiled relocation/init paths. The bot reported +924 matched code bytes and +216 matched data bytes, with new matches for lbMemory_8001564C, fn_80015184, .data, and .sdata, plus lbMemory_80015320 improved to 96.11%. The PR body says the TU reached 10/12 matched functions, with lbMemory_80014FC8 and lbMemory_80015320 still differing in saved-register allocation and instruction scheduling.
what_changed: Replaced opaque Allocator byte padding with typed fields: MemEntry x8_mem[0x83], Handle x638_heap[6], and LBMgr x6A0_mgr, while preserving sizeof(lbMemory_804318B0) == 0x6F0.
Promoted the allocator storage to the named global symbol lbMemory_804318B0 and kept local code readable through #define g_alloc lbMemory_804318B0.

Added exact static/sdata string symbols for lbmemory asserts and OSReport text, replacing __FILE__ and literal uses with labels such as lbl_803BA2C0, lbl_803BA2CC, lbl_803BA344, lbMemory_804D3788, and lbMemory_804D3794.

Implemented lbMemory_80015320 as the relocation/copy continuation routine: it updates the current destination pointer, dispatches HSD_DevComRequest for low-address copies, otherwise configures LBMgr and OSAlarm scheduling, and calls the stored completion callback when handles are exhausted.
Improved fn_80015184 by accessing &g_alloc.x6A0_mgr directly and replacing a raw tick constant/NULL callback with OSSetAlarm(..., OSMillisecondsToTicks(3), fn_80015184).
Added lbMemory_8001564C init code using ARAlloc, ARFree, ARGetSize, freelist setup for x8_mem and heap handles, initial arena handle creation, and #pragma dont_inline control.
Updated lbmemory.h to document Handle::x8_hi as context-dependent: arena high bound for heap handles and allocation size for child handles.
Removed speculative comments from lbmemory.c while keeping verified behavior notes in the header.
smart_moves: Typed the allocator layout only with size guards, using STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct MemEntry) == 0xC) and STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(lbMemory_804318B0) == 0x6F0) to prevent layout drift.
Matched data references by introducing explicit labels and SDATA strings instead of relying on compiler placement for string literals.
Kept the named global symbol required by the binary while preserving the established g_alloc source idiom through a macro alias.
Converted the alarm delay from a magic constant to OSMillisecondsToTicks(3), revealing SDK intent and contributing to fn_80015184 reaching 100%.
Used field access for the LBMgr subobject once the allocator layout was known, avoiding raw offset casts like (u8*) &g_alloc + 0x6A0.
Used #pragma dont_inline around lbMemory_8001564C as an explicit matching lever for the init routine.
Documented remaining mismatches with exact percentages and suspected causes rather than claiming full function matches.
decomp_lessons: Allocator and manager byte blobs should be converted into typed structs when repeated offsets are known; this improves readability and can unlock better matching, but must be protected by sizeof asserts.
String placement is part of matching. For asserts and OSReport calls, exact .data/.sdata labels can matter as much as control flow.
Address-named globals such as lbMemory_804318B0 can coexist with readable aliases when symbol identity and code clarity both matter.
Do not over-name fields with mixed semantics: Handle::x8_hi needed a qualified comment because it is a high bound for heap handles but a size for child allocations.
SDK macros can explain magic constants and improve codegen; OSMillisecondsToTicks(3) replaced 0x10624DD3 in this slice.
Async relocation code may split between DevCom callbacks and OSAlarm-driven chunk copies; modeling the manager struct and callback fields makes that control flow tractable.
For near-matches, saved-register allocation and instruction scheduling may remain the blocker even when control-flow and size shape look correct.
naming_conventions: Unknown or partially understood struct fields use offset-prefixed names such as x0_arenaLo, x4_arenaHi, x62C_free_mem, x638_heap, x6A0_mgr, and x6E4.
Unidentified functions, globals, and labels retain address-based names such as lbMemory_80015320, lbMemory_8001564C, lbMemory_804318B0, lbl_803BA2C0, and lbMemory_804D3794.
New local struct names used domain-style names: MemEntry, LBMgr, and Allocator.
Freelist helper macros use uppercase names: PUSH_HANDLE and POP_HANDLE.
Header comments should record verified dual-use field semantics rather than speculative questions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use explicit static char arrays with exact sizes for .data strings and SDATA char arrays for small .sdata strings when literal placement affects matches.
Pack related string literals into one labeled array when the binary appears to address substrings, as with lbl_803BA368 containing !p->size and !cancelflag.
Prefer typed subobject access after layout recovery; &g_alloc.x6A0_mgr replaced a raw 0x6A0 offset cast.
Retain low-level base+offset stores where necessary for init-code codegen, as lbMemory_8001564C still writes heap freelist links through base + 0x638 through base + 0x688.
Control compiler behavior with #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around sensitive functions.
Match async copy logic with the actual SDK APIs: HSD_DevComRequest for one path and OSCreateAlarm/OSSetAlarm with OSMillisecondsToTicks(3) for the alarm path.
Remaining stated blockers were lbMemory_80014FC8 at 99.324326% and lbMemory_80015320 at 96.106800%, with saved-register allocation and instruction scheduling still off.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
decomp-dev[bot] reported matched code increased to 67.01% (+0.02%, +924 bytes) and matched data to 39.30% (+0.02%, +216 bytes).
Bot-reported new matches were lbMemory_8001564C, fn_80015184, .data, and .sdata in main/melee/lb/lbmemory.
Bot reported lbMemory_80015320 improved from 0.00% to 96.11%.
The PR was closed with merged_at null; no rationale for closing without merge is present in the provided context.
follow_up_queries: Why was PR #2498 closed without merge, and was the lbmemory work reapplied in a later PR?
Search later history for lbMemory_80015320, lbMemory_8001564C, lbMemory_804318B0, and x6A0_mgr.
Investigate the saved-register allocation and instruction scheduling mismatches remaining in lbMemory_80014FC8 and lbMemory_80015320.
Validate Handle::x8_hi semantics across all callers before replacing offset-style field names with stronger semantic names.
Check whether the explicit .data/.sdata string labels from this PR are still required after later lbmemory changes.
